title: Primary Care PTSD Screen for DSM-5
description: A 5-item screen designed to identify individuals with probable PTSD. If no trauma exposure, score = 0.

initial_trauma_question.text: Have you ever experienced an event that was frightening, horrible, or traumatic (e.g., serious accident, assault, disaster, war, witnessing death or injury)?

questions.text: In the past month, have you had nightmares about the event(s) or thought about the event(s) when you did not want to?
In the past month, have you tried hard not to think about the event(s) or went out of your way to avoid situations that reminded you of the event(s)?
In the past month, have you been constantly on guard, watchful, or easily startled?
In the past month, have you felt numb or detached from people, activities, or your surroundings?
In the past month, have you felt guilty or unable to stop blaming yourself or others for the event(s) or any problems they may have caused?

cut_points.note: Cut-point of 4 balances sensitivity and specificity. Clinicians may adjust depending on gender and available resources.
